pattern: Working backward through the string, each position's best answer is either 'skip this character as extra' or 'jump past a dictionary word starting here', so a small suffix DP covers every split.
hints: Think right to left: let dp[i] be the minimum number of extra characters needed for the suffix s[i:].
From position i there are two kinds of moves: treat s[i] as extra and take dp[i+1] + 1, or, for every j > i where s[i:j] is a dictionary word, jump straight to dp[j].
dp[i] is the best of all those options, and dp[0] is the answer; hashing the dictionary into a set makes each 's[i:j] in dictionary' check O(1) after slicing.

edge_inputs.description: one leftover character between two matched words
three leftover characters before two matched words
overlapping dictionary words where a greedy longest-match would miss the optimal split
whole string is one dictionary word

stress.description: random strings with a dictionary built from random substrings
